doc_title: van Berg, Kowalski & Wright (2025). Learning-Styles-Matched Instruction: A Meta-Analysis of 124 Studies. Educational Psychology Review, 37(3), 612-651.
label: fabricated
reason: The document contradicts the robust scientific consensus that learning-styles-matched instruction lacks empirical support, reports an implausibly large effect size, and features unverifiable authors, DOI, and publication details.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 97
REASON: The document contradicts the overwhelming scientific consensus—supported by multiple genuine meta-analyses and reviews (e.g., Pashler et al., 2008; Rogowsky et al., 2020)—that learning-styles-matched instruction produces no meaningful achievement benefit, and the reported large effect size (d = 0.62) directly opposes this established literature; furthermore, the authors, DOI, and publication details cannot be verified and appear invented.
